narrative: OMX30 faces headwinds from a sharp US equity selloff overnight (S&P -1.62%, NASDAQ -1.98%) despite continental Europe showing resilience (DAX +0.08%, CAC +0.63%). The weighted correlation signal is -0.363%, indicating US weakness is offsetting positive European technicals. While the index trades 5.7% above its 200-MA in bull regime, the MACD is below signal with deteriorating histogram (-9.392) and ADX 32.6 shows strong bearish directional bias. US 10Y yields spiking to 4.542% will pressure Swedish tech and real estate, though banks benefit from widening NIMs. Risk-off sentiment dominates despite modest commodity stability and USD strength.
morningBrief: OMX30 likely to open modestly negative, tracking weak US overnight close and elevated yields. Watch the 3123 pivot (yesterday close) for support; critical floor at 3085 (lower Bollinger band). Exporters like Volvo B, Atlas Copco A, and Ericsson B will trade with mixed signals—USD/SEK strength (+0.48%) is positive for translation but NASDAQ selloff (-1.98%) is dragging tech sentiment. Banks (SEB A, Handelsbanken A) are only bright spot with 10Y yield +31bp widening margin expectations. Avoid chasing weakness; RSI 40.5 offers no mean reversion trigger yet. Key watch: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET—any surprise could reignite volatility. VIX at 20.6 is elevated; expect choppy consolidation in 3085–3160 range.
drivers: US equity selloff (-1.6% to -2.0%) creating negative correlation spillover
Rising US 10Y yields (+31bp) compressing valuations for growth/tech
Strong USD/SEK (+0.48%) benefiting exporters but offset by risk-off sentiment
ADX 32.6 with bearish DI bias signals downtrend persistence despite bull regime label

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield +31bp to 4.542% significantly widens net interest margin expectations; positive carry for Swedish mortgage books.
NASDAQ down 1.98% overnight; DCF valuations under compression from rising yields (4.542%); sentiment deterioration in growth stocks.
US 10Y yield spike increases Swedish mortgage refinancing costs; asset valuations face headwind from higher discount rates.
USD/SEK strength (+0.48%) supports export pricing, but global demand concerns from US equity selloff offset benefit. Brent down 0.60% marginally helps input costs.
Gold +0.17% and copper -0.62% provide mixed signals; Boliden gains from gold but copper weakness limits upside. Risk-off reduces industrial demand.
Modest risk-off rotation into defensive names; these benefit from flight-to-safety amid US volatility.

watchList.reason: 10Y yield +31bp directly expands NIM; technical support at 3123 provides entry point if OMX breaks down slightly.
watchList.catalyst: Yields, earnings surprise upside on net interest income

watchList.reason: NASDAQ -1.98% overnight; tech sentiment deteriorating; yield compression negative for growth multiples.
USD/SEK +0.48% supports export competitiveness, but demand concerns from US equity weakness create stalemate. Monitor for 3085 OMX support break.
Weak EUR/SEK sentiment (EUR/SEK +0.49%) increases sourcing costs; consumer discretionary under pressure from risk-off mood.
Gold +0.17% is modest tailwind, but copper -0.62% and risk-off sentiment limiting upside. No clear directional bias.

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET (today) — key catalyst for risk sentiment reversal. No major Swedish releases today. Monitor ECB speakers for any policy guidance.
topRisk: US yield spiral continues (10Y already +31bp); if jobless claims disappoint, recession fears spike and VIX >25. OMX 3085 support break triggers capitulation. Trade war escalation headlines remain tail risk given EU diplomatic tensions noted in newsfeed.
technicalSummary: Above 200-MA (+5.7%) but MACD below signal (hist -9.392 = bearish divergence)
ADX 32.6 with bearish DI bias indicates downtrend pressure despite bull regime label
RSI 40.55 neutral, no mean reversion trigger yet
Bollinger %B 0.31 in lower half = room to rally but momentum weak
Stochastic K=27.4, D=13.6 = oversold setup forming but not yet confirmed
-4.4% from 52W high, 82% of range = consolidation in mature bull phase
